summary: Israel announced it will not withdraw from southern Lebanon until Hezbollah is fully disarmed, while IDF forces destroyed a 200‑meter underground Hezbollah tunnel complex in the village of Maghdal Zun, southern Lebanon, containing hundreds of weapons. The IDF also eliminated a senior Hamas security officer from the Rafah brigade and a Palestinian Islamic Jihad operative in Gaza. Tensions remain high across multiple fronts, including potential Iranian retaliation.
key_points: Israel's Defense Minister Yoav Katz reiterated that Israel has no territorial ambitions in Lebanon but will not withdraw a millimeter until Hezbollah is disarmed, citing US‑approved security zones (Source: @manniefabian/58707).

IDF units 551 and Yahalom of the 91st Division destroyed a more than 200‑meter underground tunnel in Maghdal Zun, southern Lebanon, which housed hundreds of weapons and four rocket launchers aimed at Israel (Source: @New_security8200/202141).
The Israeli military reported the killing of Hamas security chief Ismail al‑Masri, a senior figure in the Rafah brigade, during a joint IDF‑Shin Bet operation (Source: @idf_telegram/24471).
A Palestinian Islamic Jihad member, Zaher Abu Salem, was killed in an Israeli strike in northern Gaza (Source: @manniefabian/58705).
An IDF reservist was severely wounded by an explosion while operating in the Lebanese village of Arnoun during Commando Brigade actions (Source: @manniefabian/58708).
Hezbollah reportedly refused a Lebanese army request to clear the Ali Taher Ridge area, where a major tunnel system is located (Source: @manniefabian/58707).
US Central Command chief Adm. Brad Cooper discussed with Israeli officials that the IDF will not withdraw from three security zones in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza (Source: @manniefabian/58707).
Qatari Foreign Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani called for understandings with neighboring Iran (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373564).

sections.executive_summary: Israel's Defense Minister stated the IDF will not withdraw from southern Lebanon until Hezbollah is fully disarmed, emphasizing long‑term presence. IDF forces destroyed a large underground Hezbollah tunnel network in Maghdal Zun, containing hundreds of weapons and rocket launchers. Simultaneously, Israeli operations eliminated senior Hamas and Islamic Jihad figures in Gaza and Rafah, while an IDF reservist was wounded in Arnoun, Lebanon.

points: Press TV commentator Mohsen Saleh claimed the United States can no longer control West Asia, asserting that Iran's state institutions now define a new regional order (Source: @presstv/196096).
Presstv reported that Iran is acquiring new missiles and mass‑producing fast‑attack craft, raising concerns in Washington (Source: @presstv/196127).
Qatari Foreign Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani said Iran is a neighboring state and called for mutual understandings (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373564).
A senior American official reportedly said the United States and Iran agreed to halt mutual attacks in the Strait of Hormuz and schedule a meeting in Doha (Source: @New_security8200/202142).

Defense Minister Yoav Katz affirmed Israel has no territorial ambitions in Lebanon but will not withdraw any distance until Hezbollah is disarmed, a stance supported by the United States and reflected in the military annex of the recent framework agreement (Source: @manniefabian/58707).

IDF units 551 and Yahalom of the 91st Division destroyed a 200‑meter underground tunnel in Maghdal Zun, southern Lebanon, which housed hundreds of weapons and four rocket launchers targeting Israeli territory (Source: @New_security8200/202141).
The IDF reported the killing of Hamas security chief Ismail al‑Masri, a senior operative in the Rafah brigade, during a joint operation with Shin Bet (Source: @idf_telegram/24471).

An IDF reservist was severely wounded by an explosion in the Lebanese village of Arnoun while the Commando Brigade was conducting operations; the cause remains under investigation (Source: @manniefabian/58708).

Israeli officials warned that Iran could launch ballistic missiles in response to actions in Lebanon, and the IDF is prepared to strike targets in Iran within two days if needed (Source: @manniefabian/58707).
US Central Command chief Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed with Israeli officials that the IDF will not withdraw from three security zones in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza (Source: @manniefabian/58707).

The Israeli military announced the killing of Palestinian Islamic Jihad operative Zaher Abu Salem in a strike in northern Gaza, describing him as a participant in the October 7 attacks (Source: @manniefabian/58705).
IDF and Shin Bet operations continue in Gaza to eliminate remaining threats, with forces positioned according to the cease‑fire agreement (Source: @idf_telegram/24471).
Hezbollah refused a Lebanese army request to clear the Ali Taher Ridge area, where a major tunnel system is located, according to Israeli Defense Minister Katz (Source: @manniefabian/58707).
IDF forces remain stationed in southern Lebanon, having not yet entered underground passages believed to house about 30 Hezbollah operatives (Source: @manniefabian/58707).

Katz stated that 73 % of villages in the eastern sector of southern Lebanon are destroyed, with almost total destruction in western and central sectors, and that roughly 600,000 Shiite residents have fled (Source: @manniefabian/58707).
Israel plans to use 500 tons of explosives to destroy a major underground Hezbollah system beneath the Beaufort Ridge (Source: @manniefabian/58707).
Israeli Defense Minister referenced that the IDF will not withdraw from the security zone in Syria as part of the three‑zone agreement (Source: @manniefabian/58707).

US‑Iran negotiations concerning a potential agreement were to be briefed to the US Congress, with the US Secretary of State and White House envoy expected to discuss the Washington‑Tehran memorandum (Source: @SharghDaily/168796).

key_developments: IDF destroyed a 200‑meter underground Hezbollah tunnel in Maghdal Zun, southern Lebanon, containing hundreds of weapons and four rocket launchers (Source: @New_security8200/202141).
Israel's Defense Minister Yoav Katz declared Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon until Hezbollah is fully disarmed, despite US approval of the security zones (Source: @manniefabian/58707).
The IDF eliminated Hamas security chief Ismail al‑Masri, a senior figure in the Rafah brigade, in a joint operation with Shin Bet (Source: @idf_telegram/24471).
Palestinian Islamic Jihad operative Zaher Abu Salem was killed in an Israeli strike in northern Gaza (Source: @manniefabian/58705).
An IDF reservist was severely wounded by an explosion in Arnoun, Lebanon, during Commando Brigade operations (Source: @manniefabian/58708).
Hezbollah refused Lebanese army entry to clear the Ali Taher Ridge tunnel area, maintaining its defensive positions (Source: @manniefabian/58707).
US‑Iran talks on halting mutual attacks in the Strait of Hormuz were reported, with a meeting scheduled in Doha (Source: @New_security8200/202142).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah retains extensive underground infrastructure in southern Lebanon, demonstrated by the large tunnel network uncovered in Maghdal Zun, posing a persistent cross‑border threat. Israeli forces remain entrenched in Lebanon, with no planned withdrawal until Hezbollah's capabilities are neutralized, indicating a protracted security posture. Simultaneously, Hamas and Islamic Jihad elements continue to be targeted in Gaza, reflecting ongoing low‑intensity conflict. Potential Iranian retaliation via ballistic missiles adds a strategic escalation risk, while US‑Iran diplomatic overtures may influence regional calculations.
sections.regional_response: The United States has signaled support for Israel's security zones in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza, and has engaged in high‑level talks with Iran to curb mutual hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz. Qatar's call for understandings with Iran suggests a desire for regional de‑escalation, though Iran's missile acquisitions and fast‑attack craft production raise concerns. Lebanese authorities' refusal to confront Hezbollah tunnels indicates limited state capacity to alter the balance of power in the south.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah tunnel activity in southern Lebanon should prioritize satellite and SIGINT assets to detect further underground expansions, especially around Ali Taher Ridge. Continued HUMINT collection on Hamas and Islamic Jihad leadership in Gaza is essential to pre‑empt retaliatory attacks. Iranian missile deployments and fast‑attack craft production warrant close naval surveillance in the Persian Gulf. Coordination with US intelligence on potential Iranian ballistic missile launches against Israel is critical for rapid response planning.
